title: PowerShell Remoting
description: Learn to enable, test, and use PowerShell remoting with PSSessions, Invoke-Command, and Enter-PSSession.

tasks.prompt: Enable PowerShell remoting on the local machine.
tasks.hint: Use Enable-PSRemoting with -Force to skip prompts.
tasks.explanation: Enable-PSRemoting configures WinRM and firewall rules for remote sessions.

tasks.prompt: Test WinRM connectivity to the remote computer 'Server01'.
tasks.explanation: Test-WSMan verifies that the WinRM endpoint is reachable.

tasks.prompt: Start an interactive remote session on 'Server01'.
tasks.explanation: Enter-PSSession opens an interactive shell on a remote computer.

tasks.prompt: Exit the current remote PowerShell session.
tasks.hint: Use Exit-PSSession to leave the remote session.
tasks.explanation: Exit-PSSession closes the interactive remoting connection.

tasks.prompt: Run 'Get-Service' remotely on 'Server01' in a single command.
tasks.explanation: Invoke-Command runs script blocks on remote machines without an interactive session.
New-PSSession creates a reusable remote connection for multiple commands.

tasks.prompt: List all active PSSessions in the current session.
tasks.explanation: Get-PSSession shows open remote sessions and their connection state.

tasks.prompt: Close and remove PSSession with ID 1.
tasks.explanation: Remove-PSSession disconnects and cleans up persistent remote sessions.
